entries.description: Null command that always returns success

entries.description: Run command in background
Define or display aliases
Mark a function for autoloading
Start bash as a login shell
Resume suspended job in background
Show all matches on first Tab press (readline variable)
Display all Readline key bindings
Run a shell builtin, bypassing shell functions
Print the current subroutine call trace
Change the current directory
Display the command that would be executed, bypassing aliases
Display the definition of a command (suitable for scripting)
Associate a completion function with a command
List all available commands for completion
Generate completion candidates from a word list
Specify a function to generate completions for a command
Define a completion function for a command
Fall back to default completion after the custom function
Don't append a space after a successful completion
Suspend the foreground job

entries.url: https://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9799919799/utilities/stty.html · susp 控制字符（默认 Ctrl+Z，挂起前台作业）

entries.description: Display the attributes and values of variables
Remove jobs from shell's job table
Print arguments to standard output
Disable a shell builtin, forcing the use of an external command
Execute arguments as a shell command
Replace the shell with a given command
Exit the shell with an optional status
Return a false (failure) exit status
Bring job to foreground
Define a function using the 'function' reserved word (bash)
officialExamples:
function hello { echo "Hello from function"; }
hello

entries.description: Define a function using the 'function' keyword (zsh)
officialExamples:
function repeat_message { for i in {1..3}; do echo "$@"; done }
repeat_message Hi

entries.description: Display or define shell functions
Parse positional parameters for options
Forget all remembered command locations
Clear the command hash table, forcing a PATH re-scan
Clear the command hash table (zsh)
Display help for builtin commands in pseudo-manpage format
Send a signal to a job or process
Perform arithmetic evaluation
Declare a local integer variable
Declare local variables within a function (zsh)
officialExamples:
myfunc() { local -A map; map[key]="value"; echo ${map[key]}; }
myfunc

entries.description: Declare local variables visible only within a function (bash)
officialExamples:
myfunc() { local tmp="value"; echo $tmp; }
tmp="global"
myfunc
echo $tmp

entries.description: Read lines from a file into an array, stripping trailing newlines
Define a POSIX-compatible shell function
Print arguments with special formatting capabilities
Format and print arguments
Print working directory
Read a line from standard input into a variable
officialExamples: printf 'Enter your name: '; read name; echo "Hello, $name"

entries.description: Return from a function or sourced script
Exit immediately if a command exits with a non-zero status
officialExamples:
#!/bin/bash
set -e
false
echo 'This will not run'

entries.description: Exit immediately if a command exits with a non-zero status
officialExamples: set -e; false; echo "This line will never be printed"

entries.description: Pipeline returns the status of the last command that failed, instead of the last command
officialExamples: set -eo pipefail; false | true; echo "This will not be printed"

entries.description: Treat unset variables as an error when substituting
Print commands and their arguments as they are executed
Enable execution tracing; print each command and its arguments before execution
officialExamples:
#!/bin/sh
set -x
echo "Debug mode on"
ls
set +x
